result.summary: Found several visual layout issues, mostly related to horizontal clipping and fixed navigation overlap on the mobile viewport.

issues.description: The top category navigation is wider than the viewport and the rightmost tab is visibly cut off, showing only part of the “Hot Games” label.
issues.suggestion: Make the category row horizontally scrollable with proper padding, or reduce tab widths so labels are not clipped at the viewport edge.

issues.description: Game tiles in the content sections are clipped on the right side, with partial cards visible and text/images cut off.
issues.suggestion: If this is intended as a horizontal carousel, add clearer spacing and scroll affordance; otherwise adjust the grid/card widths to fit within the mobile viewport.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation appears to overlap the game list content near the bottom of the screen, partially covering lower game cards.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding/margin to the scrollable content equal to the bottom nav height so content is not hidden behind it.

issues.description: The carousel arrow controls on the hero banner sit very close to the screen edges and overlap the banner artwork, making them feel cramped.
issues.suggestion: Inset the arrows slightly or place them inside a more consistent control area with adequate touch spacing.

issues.description: Some small informational “i” icons on game cards have low visibility against dark image areas and may be hard to tap accurately.
issues.suggestion: Increase icon size or contrast and ensure a large enough touch target around each icon.
